title: Brand Voice System – Complete User Guide
description: Overview Step 1: Customer Management (Foundation) Step 2: Creating Brand Voices ⭐ Save Output as Brand Voice (New Feature) Advanced Brand Voice Style Controls Database Structure Using Brand Voices in
content: Overview What is the Brand Voice System? The Brand Voice System allows you to define, store, and automatically apply a brand's unique writing style across all AI-generated content. It ensures consistency in tone, personality, vocabulary, sentence structure, punctuation rules, and call-to-action styles. Why Use Brand Voices? Problem : Maintaining consistent brand voice manually is time-consuming and error-prone. Different team members might interpret brand guidelines differently. Solution : The Brand Voice System acts as a centralized, AI-enforceable brand style guide that automatically injects brand rules into every AI generation request. Key Capabilities Manual entry of brand parameters AI generation from description AI analysis from pasted copy URL scanning to extract brand voice Pre-configured presets Granular Control Personality traits & tone style Preferred vocabulary & forbidden terms CTA style preferences Punctuation rules (Oxford comma, contractions) Step 1: Customer Management (Foundation) ⚠️ Important Before creating brand voices, you must first create a Customer . Each brand voice is tied to a specific customer/client. Creating a Customer Navigate to Dashboard → Customers Click "Add New Customer" Enter customer name (required) and optional description Click "Save" Customer Actions Add Create new customer with name & description Edit Update customer name or description inline Delete Remove customer (cascades to brand voices) Step 2: Creating Brand Voices Method 1: Manual Entry Go to Manage Customers → Select Customer → View Details Click "Add Brand Voice" Switch to "Manual" tab Fill in all fields: Name : "Main Brand Voice" Description : Brief summary Personality Traits : friendly, professional, innovative Tone Style : conversational-warm Sentence Style : clear-concise Preferred Vocabulary : Words to use Forbidden Terms : Words to avoid CTA Style : direct-action, friendly-invitation, etc. Punctuation Rules : Oxford comma, contractions, max sentence length Click "Save Brand Voice" Method 2: AI Generation from Description Click "Add Brand Voice" Stay on "AI Generate" tab Enter: Brand Voice Name (e.g., "Tech Startup Voice") Brand Description (1-2 sentences about the brand) Sample Text (optional - helps AI understand style) Click "Generate Brand Voice" Review AI-generated fields (all populated automatically) Edit any field manually if needed Click "Save Brand Voice" Method 3: Paste Copy (AI Analysis From Text) Note: You can also use the "Save as Brand Voice" button directly from generated copy output to automatically populate this field. See the Save Output as Brand Voice section below for the automated workflow. Click "Add Brand Voice" Stay on "AI Generate" tab Scroll to "Option 2: Paste Any Copy" Manually paste text content from any source: Website paragraphs or blog posts PDF documents or brochures Email content Social media captions or posts Marketing materials Any long-form content (minimum 100 characters) Click "Analyze & Generate Brand Voice" AI analyzes: Tone and personality traits Vocabulary and word choice Sentence structure and rhythm Punctuation patterns CTA style and approach All fields auto-populate with analyzed values Review and edit if needed Click "Save Brand Voice" 💡 Example Use Case Paste any text here and AI will automatically detect tone, style, vocabulary, CTA patterns, and writing rhythm. "At EcoHome, we believe in sustainable living without compromise. Our handcrafted furniture pieces tell a story—each one made from reclaimed wood sourced from local mills. We don't just sell furniture; we create heirlooms that honor the planet while bringing warmth and character to your space." AI will extract: warm tone, sustainable vocabulary, storytelling style, consultative CTA approach, and specific punctuation preferences. How Paste Copy Analysis Works: Accepts up to 10,000 characters (minimum 100 characters) No preprocessing needed—paste raw text directly Sends content to GPT-4o for deep brand voice analysis AI identifies writing patterns, personality, and style rules Returns structured JSON with all brand voice fields populated Method 4: URL Scanning (Website Analysis) Click "Add Brand Voice" Scroll to "Option 3: Scan Website for Brand Voice" Enter Website URL (e.g., https://example.com) Click "Scan & Generate Brand Voice" System fetches website content via Edge Function AI analyzes content and extracts brand voice parameters All fields auto-populate with extracted values Review and edit if needed Click "Save Brand Voice" How URL Scanning Works: Edge function fetches HTML from homepage and about page Removes scripts, styles, nav, footer Extracts clean text content (limited to 6000 chars) Sends to GPT-4o for brand voice analysis Returns structured JSON with all brand voice fields Method 5: Using Presets Click "Add Brand Voice" Switch to "Use Preset" tab Select from 6 available presets: Professional & Authoritative - For established businesses, B2B services, consulting firms Friendly & C

title: Evaluation Tools
description: Compare Feature Blend Feature Content Scoring Comparison Persistence Best Practices How to Use Compare How Blending Works Clarity Score Persuasiveness Engagement Overall Quality How Comparison Updates
content:
Compare different copy versions and blend the best elements into one optimized result. Compare Feature The Compare feature lets you view multiple copy versions side-by-side to identify the strongest elements. How to Use Compare Generate multiple copy versions (main + alternatives) Click the "Compare" button in the output section Select which versions to compare View them side-by-side with synchronized scrolling Identify differences and strengths What to Look For: Which version has the strongest opening? Which call-to-action is most compelling? Which version better addresses pain points? Which has better flow and readability? Which version aligns best with your brand voice? Blend Feature The Blend feature combines the best elements from multiple copy versions into one optimized result. How Blending Works Select 2-3 copy versions to blend The AI analyzes strengths of each version Elements are intelligently combined A new blended version is generated The blend maintains coherent flow and voice Blending Strategy: Combines the strongest opening from one version Integrates the clearest value propositions Selects the most compelling call-to-action Maintains consistent tone throughout Ensures logical flow and transitions Content Scoring Get AI-powered quality scores for your content: Clarity Score How easy your content is to understand Persuasiveness How effectively it motivates action Engagement How well it captures and maintains attention Overall Quality Comprehensive evaluation of all factors Comparison Persistence How Comparison Updates Work Important: Comparison results are static snapshots that persist exactly as generated. Once created, a comparison card remains unchanged even if you add new output cards Adding alternatives, applying voice styles, or creating new variations does NOT automatically update existing comparisons To include new outputs in a comparison, you must manually click "Compare All Outputs" again This creates a new comparison card while preserving the original comparison for reference Example Workflow: Generate 3 alternatives → Click "Compare All Outputs" → Get Comparison Card A (analyzes 3 outputs) Apply voice style to best alternative → Comparison Card A remains unchanged Create another alternative → Comparison Card A still shows original 3 outputs Click "Compare All Outputs" again → Get Comparison Card B (analyzes all 5 current outputs) Now you have both comparisons saved for reference Best Practices Generate at least 2-3 versions before comparing Use different voice styles for more variety Compare scores as well as content Blend versions with complementary strengths Always review blended content for coherence Test different blends if the first isn't perfect Click "Compare All Outputs" again after creating new variations to include them in analysis Keep multiple comparison cards to track different analysis perspectives Evaluation Tools Evaluation Tools >

  • Click the >

title: Copy Maker Overview
description: Key Features Getting Started Learn More Getting Started Core Workflows Optional Features Setup & Inputs
content:
Copy Maker is CopyZap's powerful AI copywriting tool that helps you create, improve, and optimize marketing content. Key Features Generate fresh marketing copy from scratch Improve and refine existing content Apply brand voices and voice styles Score content quality with AI-powered analysis Create multiple variations and alternatives Generate SEO metadata and optimized elements Save templates for reusable workflows Getting Started To start using Copy Maker: Navigate to the Copy Maker tab from the main menu Fill in your project details and requirements Configure optional features as needed Click Generate to create your copy Learn More Getting Started First steps and quick setup wizard walkthrough Core Workflows Create, improve, compare, and apply brand voice Optional Features Explore advanced copywriting tools Setup & Inputs Brand voice, templates, and project configuration Copy Maker Overview Copy Maker Overview > Copy Maker is CopyZap > First steps and quick setup wizard walkthrough

title: Core Workflows
description: Workflow 1: Create New Copy Workflow 2: Improve Existing Copy Workflow 3: Generate Alternatives Workflow 4: Compare & Select Workflow 5: Apply Brand Voice Real Workflow Examples Next Steps Step-by
content: TL;DR CopyZap has five core workflows: Create New Copy, Improve Existing Copy, Generate Alternatives, Apply Brand Voice, and Compare & Blend Outputs. Master these to get maximum value. Use this when: You need to create, improve, or compare copy You want consistent brand voice across content You're looking for proven workflow patterns What you'll get: 5 proven workflows for different copywriting tasks Step-by-step walkthroughs with tutorials Real-world examples for common scenarios Workflow 1: Create New Copy When to use: Starting from scratch. You have no existing copy. Step-by-Step Select Create mode at the top Fill required fields: Project Description (internal name) Product/Service Name Business Description (be specific about what you offer and who it's for) Add recommended fields: Target Audience Key Message Call to Action Tone Set Word Count (Short: 50-100, Medium: 100-200, Long: 200-400) Click Generate to create your first version Pro Tip The more detailed your Business Description, the better your output. Include: who you serve, what problem you solve, what makes you different, and key benefits. Tutorial: Generate your first output 7-step walkthrough using the Quick Prompt Wizard to generate professional copy in under 2 minutes. Note: You can edit any field after the wizard — it is a starting point, not locked in. Workflow 2: Improve Existing Copy When to use: You already have copy but want to make it better. Step-by-Step Select Improve mode at the top Fill required fields: Project Description Product/Service Name Original Copy (paste your existing content) Specify what to improve: Use Key Message to clarify main point Use Tone to change style Use Special Instructions to be explicit (e.g., "Make it more concise" or "Add more urgency") Click Generate to create your first version Common Mistake Don't paste copy and expect magic without guidance. Tell the AI what to improve using the Special Instructions field. Tutorial: Improve copy from a URL Use the Quick Prompt Wizard to extract and improve copy from a live website. Workflow 3: Generate Alternatives When to use: You have one version but want different options or approaches. Two Ways to Generate Alternatives Method 1: During Initial Generation Before clicking Generate, scroll to Optional Features section Enable Create Variants toggle Set Number of Variants (2-10) Click Generate → all variants appear together Method 2: After Generation (On-Demand) Generate your first output normally Look at the output card Click Create Alternative Copy button New alternative appears as separate output card Repeat as needed What's the difference? Each alternative uses a different angle or structure while maintaining the core message. Think of it as A/B test variations. Workflow 4: Compare & Select When to use: You have 2+ versions and want to pick the best one objectively. Tutorial: Compare versions & pick a winner Workflow 5: Apply Brand Voice When to use: Maintaining consistent style across all content, especially for agencies managing multiple clients. Setup (One-Time) Open Copy Maker Create a Customer (the client or brand) Create a Brand Voice for that customer: Option A: Paste existing copy → AI analyzes and extracts voice Option B: Describe the brand → AI generates voice profile Usage (Every Generation) Select your Customer from dropdown Select associated Brand Voice Fill form and generate normally All outputs automatically match brand voice What Brand Voice includes: Personality traits, tone style, sentence style, preferred vocabulary, forbidden terms, punctuation rules, and advanced style controls (formality, rhythm, POV, etc.). Real Workflow Examples Learn proven workflows for different content types and copywriting scenarios. New Project from Scratch Click "Quick Wizard" for guided setup Answer questions about your project Review and adjust filled fields Generate initial copy Refine based on results Save as template for similar projects Improving Existing Copy Paste existing copy into Original Copy field Select "Improve" mode Specify what to improve (clarity, engagement, etc.) Generate improved version Compare original vs improved Use blend feature to combine best of both A/B Testing Content Generate main copy with base settings Generate 2-3 alternatives with different approaches Apply different voice styles to each Score all versions Select top 2 for A/B testing Export both versions Agency Workflow Select Customer → apply Brand Voice Create new copy Generate 3 alternatives Compare & blend best elements Apply final polish with voice style Try this now: Go to Copy Maker → Practice one of these workflows to see results Next Steps Set up Brand Voice and Templates Explore optional features (SEO, GEO, scoring) Understand scoring and comparison Last updated: 2026-04-17 Core Workflows Core Workflows not-prose bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950 border border-blue-200 dark:border-blue-800 rounded-lg p-6 mb-8 text-sm font-semibold text-blue-900 dark:text-blue-

title: Credits & Billing
description: What Are Credits? Where Your Credit Balance Is Shown What Actions Consume Credits What Happens When Credits Reach 0 Best Practices to Reduce Credit Use Viewing Credit Usage History Frequently Asked Qu
content: Quick Overview Credits are the currency of CopyZap. Every AI generation consumes credits based on the AI model and features you use. Your credit balance is always visible in the top navigation bar. What Are Credits? Credits are the internal currency that powers CopyZap. When you use AI to generate, improve, or analyze copy, you consume credits based on: AI model selected: Different models have different costs (e.g., Claude Sonnet 4.5 vs GPT-3.5 Turbo) Amount of content: Longer inputs and outputs consume more credits Optional features enabled: SEO metadata, scoring, GEO optimization, and other features add to credit usage Number of variants: Generating 5 variants consumes more credits than generating 1 Note: Credits are your account balance. Every AI operation consumes credits based on the model used and the length of your content. You only need to track your credit balance — everything else is handled automatically. Where Your Credit Balance Is Shown Your current credit balance is always visible in the top navigation bar (next to your email address) when you're logged in. What you'll see: Credits Remaining: Your current available balance Color indicators: Green: Healthy balance (>1000 credits) Yellow: Low balance (100-1000 credits) Red: Very low or depleted (<100 credits) What Actions Consume Credits Every AI-powered operation in CopyZap consumes credits. Here are the main actions: Primary Generation (Copy Maker) Generating new copy or improving existing copy in Copy Maker. Typical cost: 50-300 credits depending on length and model Multi-Variant Generation Creating multiple versions in one generation (2-10 variants). Typical cost: 2-10x the base generation cost Alternative Copy Generating alternative versions of existing output. Typical cost: 40-250 credits per alternative Voice Style Application Applying voice styles (Humanize, Alex Hormozi, Steve Jobs, etc.) to outputs. Typical cost: 30-200 credits per style application Optional Features Each optional feature is a separate AI operation: SEO Metadata Generation: 40-150 credits Content Scoring: 20-80 credits GEO Scoring: 30-100 credits Headlines Generation: 25-90 credits FAQ Schema: 30-100 credits Comparison & Analysis Using Grok AI to compare and analyze multiple outputs. Typical cost: 100-400 credits depending on number of outputs Workflows Automated multi-step workflows consume credits for each step. Typical cost: Sum of all individual step costs (150-600 credits for 3-5 step workflow) Brand Voice Creation Using AI to analyze copy or generate brand voice from description. Typical cost: 40-150 credits per analysis What Happens When Credits Reach 0 When your credit balance reaches zero or goes negative: Generation is blocked: You cannot generate new copy, alternatives, or use any AI-powered features Credit Limit modal appears: A modal displays your current balance and instructs you to contact support Read-only access: You can still view saved outputs, templates, and settings, but cannot create new content No grace period: There is no "buffer" or grace credits—once you hit 0, generation stops immediately ⚠️ Important Contact support before your credits run out to ensure uninterrupted service. Check your balance regularly in the top navigation bar. Best Practices to Reduce Credit Use Choose the right AI model: Use GPT-3.5 Turbo or Claude Haiku for simple tasks instead of more expensive models. Claude Sonnet 4.5 is the default for balanced quality and cost. Disable unused optional features: Don't enable SEO metadata, scoring, or GEO optimization unless you need them for that specific generation. Generate fewer variants: Start with 1-2 variants instead of 5-10. You can always generate more alternatives later if needed. Use "Replace Input" for iterations: Instead of regenerating from scratch, use the "Replace Input" button to improve existing copy (saves on repeated input processing). Write clear, concise inputs: Vague inputs often require regeneration. Be specific to get good results on the first try. Use templates: Templates save time and reduce trial-and-error generations by reusing proven configurations. Avoid unnecessary comparisons: Grok comparison is powerful but credit-intensive. Use it only when you truly need objective analysis of 3+ outputs. Test workflows before scaling: Test workflows on single generations before using them on every project to ensure they're worth the credit cost. Viewing Credit Usage History To see detailed credit consumption: Navigate to Dashboard Click the Usage History tab View usage grouped by generation (primary generation + all related operations) Filter by date range to analyze patterns Export to CSV for detailed analysis Note: Usage History shows your credit consumption per generation. Your current balance is always visible in the top navigation bar. Frequently Asked Questions Why did my credits decrease even though generation failed? If the AI API successfully processed your request but you didn't like the result, c

title: Dashboard & History
description: Where to Find the Dashboard Dashboard Tabs Overview Usage History Tab: Understanding Your History Saved Outputs Tab: Your Content Library How Usage History Relates to Saved Outputs Deleting Outputs Co
content: Quick Overview The Dashboard is your command center for managing saved outputs, viewing usage history, and organizing past work. Access it anytime from the bottom navigation bar. Where to Find the Dashboard The Dashboard is accessible from the bottom navigation bar when you're logged in: Look for the navigation bar at the bottom of the screen Click the Dashboard link (icon: folder or chart) The Dashboard opens with three main tabs: Usage History Saved Outputs , and Manage Workflows Dashboard Tabs Overview Tab 1: Usage History View your credit consumption history, grouped by generation. See credits used, API calls, and models used. Tab 2: Saved Outputs Access all outputs you've explicitly saved from Copy Maker. Search, filter, and load past work back into the form. Tab 3: Manage Workflows View, edit, duplicate, and delete your saved workflows. (See Workflow Builder for details) Usage History Tab: Understanding Your History What You'll See The Usage History tab displays your generation history grouped by generation event. Each entry represents one generation and all related operations (alternatives, voice styles, scoring, etc.). What Each Entry Includes Each generation entry includes: Primary copy generation All optional features (SEO, scoring, GEO, etc.) Alternatives generated from that output Voice styles applied Comparison analyses Information Displayed Each row shows: Name: Auto-generated from your project description (e.g., "Homepage Hero - 2026-01-31") Date & Time: When the generation was created Credits Used: Total credits consumed for this generation and all related operations API Calls: Number of separate AI operations Models Used: Which AI models were used (e.g., Claude Sonnet 4.5, GPT-4o) Operations: Types of operations performed (generate_copy, seo_metadata, voice_style_analysis, etc.) Filtering and Searching Use the filters at the top of the Usage History tab: Date Range: Default is last 30 days; customize to view specific periods Customer Filter: View usage for specific clients (agency use case) Export to CSV: Download detailed usage report for accounting or analysis Expanding Entry Details Click any row to expand and see: Individual API calls within that entry Credits consumed per operation Model used for each operation Timestamp of each operation Saved Outputs Tab: Your Content Library What Gets Saved Here Only outputs you explicitly save appear in the Saved Outputs tab. Automatic saves do NOT happen—you must click the "Save" button on an output card in Copy Maker. ⚠️ Important If you generate copy but don't click "Save," it will NOT appear in Saved Outputs. Always save important outputs before closing Copy Maker. How to Save an Output After generating copy in Copy Maker, locate the output card Click the Save button (disk/floppy icon or "Save" text) A modal appears asking for: Title: Name your saved output (e.g., "Homepage Hero - Final Version") Description: Optional notes about this output Tags: Add keywords for easier searching later Click Save to Dashboard Output is now accessible from Dashboard → Saved Outputs tab Finding Saved Outputs The Saved Outputs tab provides multiple ways to find past work: Search bar: Search by title, description, or tags Customer filter: Filter by client/customer Date filter: View outputs from specific date ranges Tag filter: Click tags to filter by keyword Favorites: Star important outputs and filter to show only favorites Saved Output Card Actions Each saved output card shows: Title & Description: What you named it when saving Date saved: When you saved this output Customer: Which client/customer it's associated with (if any) Tags: Keywords you assigned Favorite star: Click to mark as favorite Click a saved output card to: View: Opens a modal showing all input fields and generated output Load: Restores the complete form state and output to Copy Maker (lets you edit and regenerate) Delete: Permanently removes the saved output (confirmation required) How Usage History Relates to Saved Outputs This is a common point of confusion—here's the relationship: Usage History vs Saved Outputs Usage History (automatic): Created automatically every time you generate copy Used for credit tracking and consumption history Visible in Usage History tab Cannot be deleted by users (permanent record for billing) Saved Outputs (manual): Created only when you click "Save" on an output card Used for accessing past work and loading into Copy Maker Visible in Saved Outputs tab Can be deleted anytime (does not affect usage history) Key Point: Every generation is tracked automatically in Usage History. But only outputs you manually save appear in Saved Outputs (for your reference library). Deleting Outputs Delete Individual Saved Output Navigate to Dashboard → Saved Outputs tab Find the output you want to delete Click the Delete button (trash icon) Confirm deletion in the modal Output is permanently removed from Saved Outputs Note: Deleting a saved output does NOT remove it from Usage Hist

title: Export & File Management
description: Export Formats What's Included in Exports Saving to Dashboard Best Practices Common Questions Copy as Markdown Export as HTML Save Output
content:
Learn how to export, save, and manage your generated content. Export Formats CopyZap offers two powerful export options to fit your workflow: Copy as Markdown Copies all outputs, inputs, and metadata as formatted Markdown to your clipboard. Best for: Quick sharing to Notion, Slack, GitHub, or documentation tools Preserving formatting and structure No file download needed - instant clipboard copy Works with any Markdown-compatible platform Export as HTML Downloads a professionally formatted, standalone HTML file with inline CSS styling. Best for: Client deliverables (can print to PDF from browser) Formal documentation with styled formatting Archival with readable, professional presentation Works offline once downloaded - open in any browser 💡 Tip: To create a PDF, open the HTML file in your browser and use File → Print → Save as PDF What's Included in Exports Both export formats include comprehensive content: All generated output cards (improved, alternatives, humanized versions) Original input data and form settings SEO metadata (URL slugs, meta descriptions, H1/H2/H3 variants, OG tags) if generated Content quality scores and GEO scores if available Comparison results if you've run Compare All Outputs Project metadata (timestamp, word counts, settings) Saving to Dashboard Save Output Save your generated content to the dashboard for future reference, editing, and reloading. What gets saved: All generated outputs and variants Complete form inputs and settings Relationships between outputs (alternatives, derived versions) SEO metadata, scores, and comparison results Access saved outputs: Dashboard → Saved Outputs tab → Click any card to reload into Copy Maker Best Practices For quick sharing: Use Copy as Markdown to paste into Slack, Notion, or emails For client presentations: Export as HTML and print to PDF from your browser For archiving: Save to Dashboard for reloadable copies, or export as HTML for offline backups For collaboration: Export as HTML to share with team members who don't have CopyZap access For version control: Copy as Markdown and commit to Git for tracking content changes Common Questions Can I edit exported files? Markdown: Yes, paste into any Markdown editor and edit freely. HTML: Yes, open in a text editor or HTML editor to modify the content and styling. How do I create a PDF? Export as HTML Open the HTML file in any web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) Go to File → Print Choose "Save as PDF" as the printer destination Save your PDF This gives you full control over PDF formatting using your browser's print settings. What's the difference between Save and Export? Save: Stores outputs in your CopyZap dashboard. You can reload them later to continue editing or generate more variations. Export: Creates a file or clipboard copy for use outside CopyZap. Use this for sharing, presenting, or archiving. Export & File Management Export & File Management >
  • All generated output cards (improved, alternatives, humanized versions)
  • Original input data and form settings
  • SEO metadata (URL slugs, meta descriptions, H1/H2/H3 variants, OG tags) if generated
  • Content quality scores and GEO scores if available
  • Comparison results if you >
  • For quick sharing: Use Copy as Markdown to paste into Slack, Notion, or emails
  • For client presentations: Export as HTML and print to PDF from your browser
  • For archiving: Save to Dashboard for reloadable copies, or export as HTML for offline backups
  • For collaboration: Export as HTML to share with team members who don >

title: Getting Started with CopyZap
description: What is CopyZap? When to Use CopyZap Your First Generation: Step-by-Step What Happens After Generation? Quick Tips for Better Results Next Steps Step 1: Choose Create or Improve Step 2: Configure Your
content:
Quick Start CopyZap helps you create and improve marketing copy using AI. Fill out a few fields, click Generate, and get professional copy in seconds. What is CopyZap? CopyZap is an AI-powered platform for creating marketing copy. The main tool is called Copy Maker , which generates copy based on your inputs. What makes it different: Structured inputs: Fill specific fields instead of writing prompts Consistent results: Same inputs = same quality Brand voice: Save voice guidelines and apply them automatically Templates: Save configurations and reuse them When to Use CopyZap Use CopyZap when you need to: Create new marketing copy from scratch (landing pages, emails, ads) Improve existing copy (make it clearer, more persuasive, better tone) Generate multiple variations for A/B testing Maintain consistent brand voice across all content Generate SEO metadata (meta descriptions, headings, OG tags) Your First Generation: Step-by-Step Step 1: Choose Create or Improve At the top of Copy Maker, you'll see two options: Create: Generate entirely new copy from a business description Improve: Enhance existing copy you already have → Choose based on what you have: If you're starting from scratch, pick Create. If you have a draft, pick Improve. Step 2: Configure Your Inputs Copy Maker shows all available fields. Fill in the required fields and add optional details to improve results. You don't need to fill every field — start with the essentials and add more as needed. Step 3: Fill Required Fields Three fields are always required: Project Description: Internal name (e.g., "Homepage Hero" or "Email Campaign Q4") Product/Service Name: What you're promoting (e.g., "Acme CRM Software") Business Description (Create mode) or Original Copy (Improve mode): Your main content Tip: The more specific you are in Business Description, the better your output. Step 4: Optional Fields (Recommended) These aren't required but significantly improve results: Target Audience: Who is this for? (e.g., "B2B SaaS founders") Key Message: Main point (e.g., "Save 10 hours per week") Call to Action: What action to take (e.g., "Start free trial") Tone: Professional, Friendly, Bold, etc. Word Count: Target length (Short: 50-100, Medium: 100-200, Long: 200-400) Step 5: Click Generate Click the blue Generate button. Your copy will appear below in an output card within 10-30 seconds. What Happens After Generation? After generating copy, you'll see an output card with your content. From here you can: Copy to clipboard: Click the copy button to paste elsewhere Generate alternative: Get a different version with a new angle Apply voice style: Transform copy to match specific personas (Alex Hormozi, Steve Jobs, Humanize, etc.) Modify content: Give custom instructions to adjust the copy Score content: Get quality ratings (clarity, persuasiveness, tone match) Compare versions: Use AI to compare multiple outputs and blend the best parts Quick Tips for Better Results Be specific in your Business Description. "We help companies" is vague. "We help B2B SaaS companies automate customer onboarding" is specific. Fill only the fields you need. You don't need to fill every field — the required three fields are enough to get started. Add optional fields only when they improve results for your specific copy. Use Target Audience. This single field dramatically improves relevance. Don't use all optional features at once. Start simple. Add features only when needed. Generate alternatives. First output is rarely perfect. Generate 2-3 alternatives and pick the best. Save as template. Once you find a configuration that works, save it and reuse it. Try this now: Go to Copy Maker → Generate your first output to see Copy Maker in action Next Steps Learn about Start Hub — your launchpad for three fast paths Learn core workflows — create, improve, compare Set up your inputs — brand voice, templates, project config Understand scoring and comparison Understand credits Last updated: 2026-04-17 Getting Started with CopyZap Getting Started not-prose bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950 border border-blue-200 dark:border-blue-800 rounded-lg p-6 mb-8 text-sm font-semibold text-blue-900 dark:text-blue-100 mb-2 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300 not-prose space-y-6 mb-8 border-l-4 border-blue-600 pl-4 text-xl font-bold mb-2 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300 mb-2 >→ Choose based on what you have: If you border-l-4 border-blue-600 pl-4 text-xl font-bold mb-2 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300 mb-2 >

title: Optional Features
description: Content Enhancement SEO Features Customization Options Content Scoring Headline Generation Alternative Copy SEO Metadata GEO Scoring Brand Voice Voice Styles Output Structure Tip
content: Enhance your copy generation with these powerful optional features. Content Enhancement Content Scoring Get AI-powered quality scores with detailed feedback on your generated copy. Scores evaluate clarity, persuasiveness, and engagement. Headline Generation Automatically generate multiple headline variations to find the most compelling option. Specify how many headlines you need. Alternative Copy Generate multiple versions of your copy with different approaches. Compare and choose the best one or blend elements from multiple versions. SEO Features SEO Metadata Generate optimized meta titles, descriptions, and other SEO elements. Includes URL slugs and heading variations. GEO Scoring Evaluate content for Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) - how well it performs with AI search engines and LLMs. Customization Options Brand Voice Apply custom brand voices to ensure consistency across all your content. Extract voices from existing content or create them manually. Voice Styles Apply pre-defined communication styles modeled after well-known personalities or writing approaches. Output Structure Control how your content is formatted - paragraphs, bullet points, sections, or custom structures. Tip Start with a few optional features and gradually add more as you become familiar with them. Not every feature is needed for every project. Optional Features Optional Features

title: Generated Output
description: Output Components Working with Output On-Demand Generation Export Options Main Copy Quality Scores Headlines SEO Metadata Alternative Versions Copy as Markdown Export as HTML Pro Tip
content: Understanding and working with the content generated by CopyZap. Output Components After generation, you'll see several components: Main Copy The primary generated content based on your inputs and requirements. Quality Scores AI-powered evaluation of your content's quality, clarity, and effectiveness (if enabled). Headlines Multiple headline variations optimized for engagement (if enabled). SEO Metadata Meta titles, descriptions, URL slugs, and other SEO elements (if enabled). Alternative Versions Additional copy variations created with different approaches. Working with Output You can perform several actions on generated content: Copy: Copy any section to your clipboard with one click Edit: Make inline edits to refine the content Export: Download as HTML or copy as Markdown Save: Save to your dashboard for future reference Generate Alternatives: Create new variations of specific sections Apply Voice Style: Transform content with different communication styles Score: Get quality evaluation and improvement suggestions Compare: Compare multiple versions side-by-side On-Demand Generation Generate additional content elements on-demand: More headline variations Alternative copy versions Content refinements Voice style variations SEO metadata variations Export Options CopyZap offers two export formats designed to cover all your needs: Copy as Markdown Instant clipboard copy with formatted Markdown Perfect for Notion, Slack, GitHub, and documentation Export as HTML Styled HTML file for professional presentation Print to PDF from browser for client deliverables Need a PDF? Export as HTML, open in your browser, and use File → Print → Save as PDF. This gives you full control over PDF formatting. Pro Tip Always review and refine generated content before using it. The AI provides a strong starting point, but your expertise and judgment are essential for final quality. title: Project Setup Guide
description: Essential Information Choosing Your Settings Best Practices Tone Word Count Language
content:
Learn how to set up your copywriting projects effectively for the best results. Essential Information For best results, provide the following information: Project Description: A brief overview of what you're creating Product/Service Name: The name of what you're promoting Target Audience: Who you're writing for Key Message: The main point you want to communicate Call to Action: What you want readers to do Choosing Your Settings Tone Select the tone that matches your brand and audience (e.g., Professional, Casual, Persuasive). Word Count Choose an appropriate length for your content type. Use "AI Decide" for automatic optimization. Language Select the language for your output. CopyZap supports multiple languages. Best Practices Start with the Quick Prompt Wizard if you're unsure Use clear, specific descriptions Provide context about your business and industry Include competitor information when relevant Save successful configurations as templates Project Setup Guide Project Setup >

  • Target Audience: Who you > Choose an appropriate length for your content type. Use >

title: Quick Prompt Wizard
description: How It Works When to Use It Tips
content:
The Quick Prompt Wizard is a guided workflow that helps you generate high-quality copy quickly by answering simple questions. How It Works Click the "Quick Wizard" button in Copy Maker Answer guided questions about your project The wizard fills in form fields automatically Review and adjust the generated configuration Click Generate to create your copy When to Use It The Quick Prompt Wizard is ideal for: Starting a new project from scratch When you're not sure which fields to fill in Quickly generating copy without technical knowledge Exploring what CopyZap can do Tips Be specific in your answers for better results You can always modify the wizard's suggestions Save successful configurations as templates The wizard learns from your preferences over time Quick Prompt Wizard Quick Prompt Wizard >

title: Recommended Settings
description: General Recommendations Content Type Settings Blog Posts Social Media Posts Landing Pages Email Marketing Remember
content: Best practice settings for different types of content and use cases. General Recommendations Fill in the required fields plus target audience for most projects Use optional fields (Pain Points, Competitor URLs, etc.) when you need finer control Click Score on output cards to track quality improvements Save successful configurations as templates Use brand voices for consistent messaging Content Type Settings Blog Posts Word Count: 1500-2500 words Tone: Informative or Educational Enable SEO Metadata Generate Headlines (3-5 variations) Include section breakdown Social Media Posts Word Count: 50-150 words Tone: Casual or Engaging Generate multiple alternatives Apply voice styles for variety Focus on emotional connection Landing Pages Word Count: 300-800 words Tone: Persuasive Enable content scoring Generate headlines Include compelling CTA Email Marketing Word Count: 150-300 words Tone: Friendly or Professional Generate subject line variations Apply brand voice Clear call-to-action Remember These are starting points. Adjust based on your specific audience, brand, and goals. Save your optimized configurations as templates for future use. Recommended Settings Recommended Settings

title: Start Hub
description: What is Start Hub? When Does Start Hub Appear? How to Open Start Hub Manually The Three Start Paths Controlling Start Hub Visibility Common Questions Tips for Using Start Hub Related Help Topics I clo
content: Quick Overview The StartHub is your launchpad for creating marketing copy. It appears when you first log in and provides three fast paths to get started with CopyZap Copy Maker. What is Start Hub? The Start Hub modal is an onboarding feature that helps you choose the best way to begin creating content. Instead of facing a blank form, Start Hub presents three clear paths tailored to different workflows and experience levels. The Start Hub button (with a rocket icon) is also available in the main menu, so you can access it anytime you need a fresh start or want to try a different approach. When Does Start Hub Appear? The Start Hub modal appears automatically in two situations: First login: When you first sign in to CopyZap, Start Hub greets you and helps you choose your first workflow App load with empty form: If "Show Start Hub on app load" is enabled in your preferences, it appears when you load the app with an empty Copy Maker form How to Open Start Hub Manually You can open the Start Hub modal anytime from the main menu: Look for the Start Hub button in the top navigation bar Click the orange button with the rocket icon and "Start Hub" label The Start Hub modal will open, showing all three start paths 💡 Tip: The Start Hub button is always visible in the main menu when you're in Copy Maker, making it easy to switch workflows mid-session. The Three Start Paths Start Hub offers three distinct ways to begin, each optimized for different use cases: 1. Start with Copy Wizard A guided, outcome-driven flow perfect for new users or when you want quick results without thinking about technical details. Two wizard paths: Make new copy: Create fresh content from scratch by answering simple questions about your project Improve existing copy: Paste your current content and let the wizard help you enhance it Learn more about Getting Started → 2. Start with Copy Form Manual control with varying complexity levels. For experienced users who know exactly what they want. Three complexity levels: Quick: Essential fields only (primary prompt, tone, length) — fastest path to generation Standard: Balanced control with core inputs plus SEO fields — good for most projects Advanced: Full control with all panels open including GEO optimization, competitors, and analysis options Learn more about Project Setup and form fields → 3. Start from a Template Load a saved template to reuse proven configurations. Perfect for recurring workflows or when you've already optimized settings for a specific content type. What happens: Opens the Template Picker showing your saved templates Select a template to instantly load all saved settings All form fields, optional features, and preferences are pre-filled Just update any specific details and generate Learn more about Setup & Inputs → Controlling Start Hub Visibility At the bottom of the Start Hub modal, you'll find a toggle: "Show Start Hub on app load" Enabled (default): Start Hub appears automatically when you load the app with an empty form Disabled: Start Hub won't appear automatically, but you can still access it via the Start Hub button in the main menu Your preference is saved to your account and persists across sessions. You can change this setting anytime. Common Questions I closed Start Hub — how do I get it back? Click the Start Hub button (orange button with rocket icon) in the main menu at the top of the screen. It's always available when you're in Copy Maker. Which option should I choose? Here's a quick guide: New to CopyZap? Start with Copy Wizard → Make new copy Want to improve existing content? Start with Copy Wizard → Improve existing copy Know exactly what settings you need? Start with Copy Form (Quick, Standard, or Advanced) Repeating a proven workflow? Start from a Template Does using Start Hub cost credits? No. Opening the Start Hub modal and selecting a start path is completely free. Credits are only consumed when you click the Generate button to create copy. Start Hub simply helps you set up your form — the actual AI generation is what uses credits. Learn more about Credits & Billing → Can I change my mind after selecting a path? Yes! Start Hub just sets up your form. You can: Click the Start Hub button again to choose a different path Manually adjust any form fields after Start Hub closes Switch between wizard and form modes anytime Why doesn't Start Hub show my brand voices? Start Hub focuses on choosing how to start, not specific content settings. After selecting a path, you can apply your brand voice in the Copy Maker form using the Brand Voice selector or by loading a template that has your brand voice pre-configured. Learn more about Brand Voice System → Tips for Using Start Hub Experiment with different paths: Try all three options to discover which workflow suits your style Use templates for efficiency: Once you find settings that work, save them as templates and access them via Start Hub Don't overthink it: Start Hub is just a helper — you can always adjust settin

title: Templates & Reuse
description: What Are Templates? Creating Templates Loading Templates Template Types Best Practices Managing Templates Personal Templates Public Templates Quick Start Templates
content:
Save time by creating and reusing templates for your most common copywriting tasks. What Are Templates? Templates are saved configurations of form settings that can be quickly loaded and reused. They capture all your preferences including: Tone, language, and word count settings Selected optional features Brand voice and voice style preferences Output structure and format Special instructions and constraints Creating Templates Configure Copy Maker with your desired settings Click the "Save Template" button Give your template a descriptive name Add a description to remember what it's for Optionally make it public to share with other users Loading Templates To load a saved template: Click "Load Template" in Copy Maker Browse your personal templates or public templates Select the template you want to use The form will populate with all saved settings Modify any fields as needed for your specific project Template Types Personal Templates Templates you create for your own use. Only visible to you. Public Templates Templates shared by other users or provided by CopyZap. Great for discovering new approaches. Quick Start Templates Pre-configured templates for common use cases like blog posts, social media, emails, and ads. Best Practices Use descriptive names that indicate the template's purpose Create separate templates for different content types Update templates when you find better configurations Share successful templates to help other users Organize templates by category or project type Managing Templates Access your template library from the Dashboard's Templates tab. From there you can: View all your saved templates Edit template names and descriptions Delete templates you no longer need Toggle templates between public and private See when templates were created and last used Templates & Reuse Templates & Reuse >
  • Configure Copy Maker with your desired settings
  • Click the button
  • Give your template a descriptive name
  • Add a description to remember what it >
  • Click >
  • Use descriptive names that indicate the template > Access your template library from the Dashboard list-disc list-inside space-y-2 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300 ml-4

title: Common Mistakes & Troubleshooting
description: Common Mistake #1: Vague Business Description Common Mistake #2: Enabling All Optional Features Common Mistake #3: Conflicting Instructions Common Mistake #4: Not Using Target Audience Common Mistake
content: Quick Fixes Most problems come from overusing features or providing conflicting instructions. When in doubt: simplify, be specific, and generate alternatives. Common Mistake #1: Vague Business Description ❌ Wrong: "We help businesses grow." ✅ Right: "We help B2B SaaS companies reduce customer churn by 40% through automated onboarding workflows and proactive customer success alerts. Our platform integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, and Intercom." Why this matters: AI needs context. Specific inputs = specific outputs. Generic inputs = generic outputs. What to include: Who you serve (be specific about industry, role, company size) What problem you solve (quantify if possible) How you solve it (your approach/method) What makes you different (key differentiators) Common Mistake #2: Enabling All Optional Features New users often enable every toggle thinking it improves results. It doesn't. ⚠️ What Happens: Generate SEO Metadata: Adds extra processing time (use only when you need SEO elements) Generate Scores: Adds extra API call (use sparingly for evaluation, not every generation) Prioritize Word Count: Strict mode can sacrifice quality for precision (use only when exact length matters) Force Keyword Integration: Can make copy feel unnatural if overused Force Elaborations & Examples: Adds fluff to hit word count (use only for educational content) Solution: Start with no optional features. Generate. If output is good, you're done. Add features only when you need them. Common Mistake #3: Conflicting Instructions Telling the AI to do contradictory things produces confused output. ❌ Conflicting Example: Tone: "Professional" Language Style Constraints: "Avoid jargon, Simple vocabulary" Special Instructions: "Use industry-specific technical terminology" Word Count: Short (50-100 words) Force Elaborations & Examples: Enabled Why this fails: Can't be professional AND simple. Can't elaborate AND be short. Can't avoid jargon AND use technical terms. Solution: Pick one direction and stick to it. If you want professional tone, don't force simple vocabulary. Common Mistake #4: Not Using Target Audience This single field has massive impact. Skipping it is the #1 missed opportunity. Example Impact: Without Target Audience: "Our software saves time and increases efficiency." With Target Audience = "busy CFOs at mid-market companies": "Free up 15 hours per month that you're currently spending on manual financial reporting. Get board-ready reports in minutes, not days." Be specific: ✅ "E-commerce founders doing $100K-$500K/month who struggle with inventory management" ❌ "Business owners" Common Mistake #5: Expecting First Output to Be Perfect Copy Maker is a tool, not magic. Professional copywriters iterate. You should too. Professional Workflow: Generate first version Read it critically Generate 2-3 alternatives Identify what works in each Use Compare & Blend to combine best parts Apply final polish Expectation calibration: Plan for 3-5 generations minimum for important copy. Common Mistake #6: Skipping Key Fields for Complex Copy For important projects, filling only the minimum required fields often produces generic output. The more context you provide, the better your results. Fields that most improve quality: Customer/Brand Voice, Target Audience, Pain Points, Industry/Niche, Output Structure Result when skipped: Generic output without brand consistency or strategic positioning Solution: For important copy, fill in brand voice, target audience, and key message at minimum. Use the optional features section for SEO, scoring, and other enhancements. Common Mistake #7: Not Saving Templates Manually filling same fields repeatedly wastes time. What to save as templates: Product launch announcement (reuse for every launch) Newsletter format (reuse weekly) Landing page structure (reuse for new pages) Client-specific setup (Customer + Brand Voice + typical settings) How: After successful generation, click "Save as Template", give it a name, select category. Next time, load template and only update content-specific fields. Troubleshooting: Output Feels "Off" If output is too generic: → Add Target Audience → Be more specific in Business Description → Add Target Audience Pain Points → Include competitor differentiation If output is too formal/stiff: → Change Tone to "Friendly" or "Creative" → After generation, apply "Humanize" voice style → Add to Special Instructions: "Write like you're talking to a friend" If output is too casual: → Change Tone to "Professional" → In Language Style Constraints, select "Avoid contractions" → Apply "Professional Expert" voice style after generation If output is wrong length: → System automatically revises if dramatically outside range → Enable "Prioritize Word Count" for strict adherence → If still off, use Modify Content with instruction "Make this exactly X words" If output uses forbidden terms: → Add to Excluded Terms field → If using Brand Voice, add to Forbidden Terms in brand voice settings → Use Mo

title: Workflow Builder
description: What Is a Workflow? Where to Access the Workflow Builder Workflow Builder Interface Available Workflow Actions How to Create a Workflow Using a Workflow in Copy Maker Editing and Managing Workflows Co
content: Quick Overview The Workflow Builder lets you automate multi-step copy generation processes. Define a sequence of actions (like generate → apply voice → create alternative) and execute them with a single click. What Is a Workflow? A workflow is an automated sequence of copy transformation steps that execute automatically after your primary generation completes. Example workflow: Generate primary copy Create an alternative version (different angle) Apply brand voice to the original Apply "Humanize" voice style to the alternative Result: 4 outputs from 1 "Generate" click Why Use Workflows? Save time: Automate repetitive multi-step processes Consistency: Ensure same steps execute every time Client deliverables: Generate complete packages (original + alternatives + voice styles) in one click Reusability: Create once, use on every project Where to Access the Workflow Builder Navigate to the Workflow Builder: Click Dashboard in the bottom navigation bar Switch to the Manage Workflows tab Click Create Workflow to open the Workflow Builder Alternative direct access: Navigate to /manage-workflows in your browser Workflow Builder Interface Two-Panel Layout The Workflow Builder has two main areas: Left Panel: Action Library Available action types you can add to your workflow: Create Alternative Copy: Generate a new version with different angle Apply Voice Style: Transform copy to match a specific voice (brand voice or persona) Right Panel: Workflow Canvas Your workflow steps in execution order. Drag actions from the library, reorder steps, and configure each action. Workflow Metadata At the top of the builder, configure: Workflow Name: Descriptive name (e.g., "Standard Client Deliverable") Description: Optional notes about purpose or use case Customer: Associate with specific client (optional, for agency workflows) Public: Make visible to other users (your choice—does not share edit access) Available Workflow Actions 1. Create Alternative Copy Purpose: Generate a new version with different angle, phrasing, or approach. Configuration: Target: Which content to transform (Original, Alternative 1, Alternative 2, etc.) Use Case: Generate multiple versions for A/B testing or client selection. 2. Apply Voice Style Purpose: Transform copy to match a specific brand voice or famous persona. Configuration: Target: Which content to transform Voice Style: Choose from: Humanize (make less AI-like) Brand voices you've created Famous personas: Alex Hormozi, Steve Jobs, Seth Godin, Gary Vaynerchuk, Donald Miller, Eugene Schwartz Use Case: Generate copy in multiple brand voices or test different personas for audience fit. How to Create a Workflow Step 1: Open Workflow Builder Navigate to Dashboard → Manage Workflows → Create Workflow Step 2: Name Your Workflow Give it a descriptive name like "Blog Post Package" or "Client Launch Workflow" Step 3: Add Actions from Library Drag actions from the left panel to the canvas, or click "Add" buttons: Click Create Alternative Copy → Added as Step 1 Click Apply Voice Style → Added as Step 2 Click Apply Voice Style again → Added as Step 3 Step 4: Configure Each Step Click each step to configure: Step 1 (Alternative): Set Target = "Original" Step 2 (Voice Style): Set Target = "Original", Voice = "Your Brand Voice" Step 3 (Voice Style): Set Target = "Alternative 1", Voice = "Humanize" Step 5: Reorder If Needed Drag steps up or down to change execution order. Steps execute sequentially from top to bottom. Step 6: Save Workflow Click Save . Your workflow is now available in Copy Maker. Using a Workflow in Copy Maker Fill out Copy Maker form as usual Enable the "Use Workflow" toggle (in the Special Features or Optional Features section) Select your workflow from the dropdown Click Generate Wait for primary generation + workflow steps to complete (30-60 seconds for 3-5 steps) All outputs appear in separate cards (Original + workflow-generated outputs) ⚠️ Sequential Execution Workflow steps execute one at a time (not in parallel). A 5-step workflow may take 30-60 seconds total. Plan accordingly for time-sensitive projects. Editing and Managing Workflows Edit Existing Workflow Navigate to Dashboard → Manage Workflows Find the workflow you want to edit Click Edit Workflow Builder opens with existing configuration Make changes (add/remove/reorder steps) Click Save Duplicate Workflow Find the workflow in Manage Workflows Click Duplicate A copy is created with " (Copy)" suffix Edit the duplicate as needed Use Case: Create variations of workflows without modifying the original (e.g., "Client Deliverable - Standard" and "Client Deliverable - Premium"). Delete Workflow Find the workflow in Manage Workflows Click Delete Confirm deletion Workflow is permanently removed Warning: Deleted workflows cannot be recovered. Duplicate before deleting if you might need it later. Common Workflow Examples Example 1: Standard Deliverable Package Use Case: Agency providing 3 versions to client for selection Generate primary copy

title: Workflows & Examples
description: Quick Start Workflows Industry-Specific Workflows Learn More New Project from Scratch Improving Existing Copy A/B Testing Content E-commerce SaaS Content Marketing Agency
